parts.name: Couture Cast API
parts.stack_summary: Node.js 24, TypeScript 5.9, NestJS 11, Prisma 6.19, BullMQ 5, Redis, Socket.io 4, Expo Server SDK, PostHog, and OpenTelemetry

parts.name: Couture Cast Web
parts.stack_summary: Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Tailwind CSS 3.4, shared Zod API contracts, and PostHog

parts.name: Couture Cast Mobile
parts.stack_summary: Expo 54, Expo Router 6, React Native 0.81, React 19, TypeScript 5.9, shared Zod API contracts, Expo Notifications, and PostHog

parts.name: Couture Cast Database
parts.stack_summary: PostgreSQL on Supabase, Prisma 6.19 schema and client, ordered SQL migrations, row-level security, and deterministic TypeScript seeds

parts.name: Couture Cast Shared Packages
parts.stack_summary: TypeScript npm workspaces for Zod and OpenAPI contracts, generated Fetch SDK, feature configuration, common utilities, test factories, k6 helpers, and ESLint policy

integration_points.protocol: HTTPS JSON with cookies and intended Bearer authentication
integration_points.summary: The Next.js client calls public and protected API v1 routes directly or through same-origin rewrites.

integration_points.protocol: HTTPS JSON with intended Bearer authentication
integration_points.summary: The Expo client resolves a configured API origin and uses generated-client or direct-fetch adapters.
API repositories and workers use the generated Prisma client against the schema and committed SQL migrations.

integration_points.protocol: npm workspaces, TypeScript, Zod, OpenAPI, and generated Fetch SDK
integration_points.summary: Canonical HTTP, realtime, polling, and analytics contracts are shared with the API and both client applications.

integration_points.protocol: BullMQ queues, Redis cache, and Redis pub/sub
integration_points.summary: The standalone API worker schedules and consumes jobs while sharing Redis-backed queues, caches, and alert relay channels with the HTTP process.

integration_points.protocol: Socket.io private rooms with HTTPS event-polling fallback
integration_points.summary: The API can emit validated alert events over Socket.io, while persisted events support timestamp-cursor HTTP polling.
Worker-side alert fan-out can send Expo push batches when realtime delivery is unavailable.
API, web, and mobile send analytics through app-specific adapters, and the API also reads remote feature flags with database and code fallbacks.
The long-running API exports traces and periodic metrics to configured Grafana or local LGTM OTLP endpoints.
